title: Quantinuum, Rolls-Royce, Riverlane, and EPCC Partner to Accelerate Fluid Dynamics Simulations - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Quantinuum, Rolls-Royce, Riverlane, and EPCC (University of Edinburgh) launched a multi-year collaboration to move gas-turbine computational fluid dynamics algorithms — previously validated only on classical emulators for nearly five years — onto Quantinuum's 98-qubit Helios trapped-ion hardware, supporting the UK's 'teraQuOp' quantum strategy goal.

themes.theme: Quantum-accelerated industrial CFD moves from emulation to real hardware

insights.claim: Quantinuum (NASDAQ: QNT), Rolls-Royce, Riverlane, and EPCC are shifting a nearly five-year-old hybrid quantum-classical algorithm collaboration for gas turbine computational fluid dynamics from classical emulators onto physical quantum hardware, starting with Quantinuum's 98-qubit trapped-ion Helios platform.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The transition from emulator-only to real hardware after five years of preparation is a concrete signal that quantum-accelerated CFD is approaching a real validation/benchmarking phase for an industrial (aerospace/turbine) use case, not just theoretical research.
insights.quote: For nearly five years, these three partners have co-developed and refined hybrid algorithms using classical emulators. Under this new agreement, the consortium will begin executing these algorithms on physical quantum hardware
insights.locator: Transitioning from Emulation to Physical Hardware

insights.claim: The consortium plans to scale beyond Quantinuum's current 98-qubit Helios system to future Quantinuum platforms named Sol and Apollo, indicating a defined multi-generation hardware roadmap tied to this specific industrial partnership.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Names Quantinuum's next-generation hardware roadmap (Sol, Apollo) as the target platforms for scaling industrial CFD workloads — a marker to track Quantinuum's hardware release cadence against real commercial use-case deployment.
insights.quote: with future scaling planned for upcoming systems like Sol and Apollo
insights.locator: Transitioning from Emulation to Physical Hardware

insights.claim: The project directly supports the UK Government's strategic goal of fielding 'teraQuOp' systems capable of executing one trillion error-free quantum operations, with EPCC (UK National Supercomputing Centre) leading compilation and hybrid workload partitioning between classical and quantum resources.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Ties a specific industrial partnership to a named UK national quantum computing performance target (teraQuOp), useful for tracking UK government-industry alignment and potential future public funding follow-through.
insights.quote: The multi-year project directly supports the UK Government’s mission to field “teraQuOp” systems capable of executing one trillion error-free quantum operations.

insights.claim: This hardware-focused collaboration complements Rolls-Royce's separate, ongoing software work with Classiq integrating quantum linear solvers into iterative CFD environments.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Rolls-Royce is running parallel quantum-CFD tracks (hardware via Quantinuum, software via Classiq), indicating the company is hedging across multiple quantum computing vendors rather than committing to a single stack.
insights.quote: This hardware-focused collaboration builds on Rolls-Royce’s ongoing software exploration in this domain, complementing their recent work with Classiq to integrate quantum linear solvers into iterative CFD environments.
